name: is_end_true_for_some
reason: If an iterator wrapping Some(k) reports is_end as true, non-end iterators would be indistinguishable from end iterators

name: is_end_false_for_none
reason: If a None-iterator reports is_end as false, callers would attempt to dereference a nonexistent key

name: new_get_roundtrip_fails
reason: If get_spec on new_spec(k) does not return k, the accessor loses the stored key value

name: get_conflates_distinct_keys
code:
proof fn phi_4_get_conflates_distinct_keys<K: KeyTrait + VerusClone>(k1: K, k2: K)
    requires
        !(k1 == k2),
    ensures
        *KeyIterator::<K>::new_spec(k1).get_spec() == *KeyIterator::<K>::new_spec(k2).get_spec(),
{
}
reason: If get_spec maps distinct keys to the same value, the iterator cannot faithfully distinguish different keys

name: is_end_indistinguishable_some_none
reason: If is_end_spec returns the same value for Some and None variants, the end-of-iteration check is completely broken
